title: Epistemic Transport Protocol v1
description: A model-agnostic, vendor-agnostic standard for attaching epistemic metadata to AI-generated claims. ETP ensures that when information moves between AI agents, memory systems, or compression layers, its confidence level travels with it. Credence is the reference implementation.

EpistemicZone.description: Confidence zone. LOW = explicit uncertainty, preserve verbatim. MEDIUM = borderline, trim but do not compress. HIGH = epistemically resolved, safe to summarise.

EpistemicEvent.description: A single event in a constraint's certainty trajectory.

event_type.description: register=first observation; scout=auto-extracted by classifier; chat_update=confidence revised mid-session; verify=confirmed with external evidence; contradict=new claim conflicts with prior verification.

j_score.description: Epistemic confidence at this event (0=maximally uncertain, 1=maximally confident).

EpistemicConstraint.description: A single uncertain claim tracked across sessions and agent hops.

constraint_id.description: Globally unique constraint identifier (UUID or slug).

session_id.description: Session or conversation the constraint originated in.

content.description: The uncertain claim verbatim, as stated by the user or extracted by Scout.

j_score.description: Confidence score at time of registration.

verified.description: True once the claim has been confirmed against an authoritative source.

verified_value.description: The confirmed factual value. Null until verified=true.

EpistemicEnvelope.description: Provenance wrapper for AI-generated content in multi-agent pipelines. Travels with content as it moves between agents, compression layers, and memory systems. Trust degrades with each agent hop.

content.description: The AI-generated text this envelope describes.

j_score.description: Linguistic assertiveness score at generation time.

source.description: Identifier of the agent or system that generated this content.

verified.description: True if the claim has been confirmed against an external source.

chain_depth.description: Number of agent hops since the original generation. Trust degrades per hop.

trust_score.description: Degraded confidence: j_score minus chain_depth penalty minus source penalty.

should_verify.description: True when trust_score < 0.40 and content is not verified. Downstream agents must verify before acting.

safe_to_compress.description: True only when zone=HIGH, trust sufficient, and no uncertainty markers detected.

uncertainty_preserved.description: True when this content was explicitly kept verbatim by a faithfulness probe or PRESERVE decision.

content_type.description: Content type — affects J-score interpretation (code/error cap at MEDIUM max).

EpistemicLedger.description: Full epistemic state for a session. Accessible via MCP Resource at epistemic://session/{session_id}/ledger.

AlignmentWarning.description: Fired by the Output Alignment Layer (Governor) when a generated response is more confident than the ledger warrants.

constraint_content.description: The unverified constraint the response discussed.

ledger_zone.description: Confidence recorded in ledger (LOW or MEDIUM).

overlap_words.description: Content words linking response to constraint.

suggested_caveat.description: Caveat injected into next turn's system prompt.

description: ETP document root — wrap any of the above definitions.

x-etp-metadata.design_principle: Only epistemically resolved content is safe to compress. Uncertain content must survive verbatim through every compression, agent handoff, and memory operation.
x-etp-metadata.rationale: Every AI system today passes information between agents by value. Nobody passes it by epistemic weight. ETP proposes to fix this: model-agnostic epistemic metadata that travels with every piece of AI-generated information through every hop of every pipeline.
